messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Single Cell Data Analysis with R and Python
messages.subject_clean: Single Cell Data Analysis with R and Python
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Explore cutting-edge methods for analyzing complex biological data at the single-cell level. Single Cell Data Analysis 3.0 is a 2-day seminar livestreaming May 25, led by Nikolay Oskolkov (Lund University). This workshop provides crucial computational tools and methodologies for high-level single-cell data interpretation, enabling researchers to explore complex biological systems at the most granular scale. Participants will gain hands-on experience in state-of-the-art techniques for processing and analyzing single-cell RNA sequencing data, which is essential for deciphering cellular complexity and understanding genetic heterogeneity. These robust data analysis methods, implemented in R and Python, can be directly applied to advance genetic research by uncovering cell-type specific gene expression patterns and disease mechanisms.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/single-cell-data-analysis-30) to reserve your place, and feel free to circulate this with colleagues and students who may be interested!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Only 2 Seats Left – Network Analysis in R (4–7 May )
messages.subject_clean: Only 2 Seats Left – Network Analysis in R (4–7 May )
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
Only 2 seats remain for our upcoming online course: Network Analysis in R

Dates: 4?7 May 2026
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/network-analysis-in-r/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/network-analysis-in-r/ ) 
 
This course provides a practical introduction to network analysis using R, with a strong focus on hands-on implementation and real data applications (especially in the life sciences, but transferable to other fields).
Across four sessions, you will learn how to:
 
? Build and manipulate network objects in R (using igraph and related tools)
? Explore node-, edge-, and network-level statistics (centrality, cohesion, topology)
? Detect and evaluate network communities and clusters
? Apply statistical testing and simulation approaches for network data
? Work with random graphs, small-world models, and preferential attachment
? Visualize networks using best practices with ggraph and tidygraph, including interactive approaches
 
Each day combines short theoretical introductions with live coding exercises and guided practice problems, ensuring you can immediately apply what you learn.
 
 
Best regards,
Carlo

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Last chance: Exploratory Multivariate Analysis in R for Genetics
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: Exploratory Multivariate Analysis in R for Genetics
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Last chance - Explore advanced data analysis techniques to enhance your research capabilities and derive sharper empirical claims. Exploratory Multivariate Analysis in R is a 2-day seminar livestreaming May 7 and 20 with SMCS (Universit? Catholique de Louvain). This seminar introduces exploratory multivariate analysis with practical R implementation, focusing on methods like PCA, EFA, and clustering. Researchers in genetics can apply these techniques to reduce dimensionality in high-throughput biological data, discover latent genetic structures, and integrate diverse data types for more robust analyses. The instruction emphasizes reproducible R workflows to enhance inference, improve the clarity of published results, and strengthen methodological justifications in grant proposals and manuscripts.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/exploratory-multivariate-analysis-in-r) to register now, and we'd appreciate you sharing this with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Gene Set Enrichment Analysis in R course
messages.subject_clean: Gene Set Enrichment Analysis in R course
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
 
We?re excited to announce our upcoming online course ?Gene Set Enrichment Analysis in R?, taking place 11?15 May!
 
 
 
This practical course offers a comprehensive introduction to gene set enrichment analysis methods widely used in transcriptomics, proteomics, and multi-omics workflows. You?ll learn how to run, interpret, and report enrichment analyses using real-world examples and tools, with the flexibility to work on your own data.
 
 
 
What you will learn:
 

Core concepts and statistical principles of gene set enrichment

How to select and use gene set databases and annotation resources

Over-representation analysis (ORA) and ranking-based methods like GSEA

Advanced visualization and interpretation of results

Applications in single-cell and spatial omics data
 
Course format:
 Five afternoons (14:00?19:00 Berlin time) of lectures, hands-on exercises, and Q&A, fully online to support international participation.
 
Find the full program and register here:[ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/gse-in-r/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/gse-in-r/ )
 
 
 
Best regards,
 
Carlo
 
 
 
 
 
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Courses on Spatial Omics in 2026
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We are pleased to announce two upcoming online courses focused on spatial omics, designed to provide both foundational training and advanced analytical skills in this rapidly evolving field.
 
1 -  Spatial Omics in R/Bioconductor
Dates:  18?20 May 
Instructor: Dr. Stefano Mangiola (South Australian immunoGENomics Cancer Institute, Australia)
 
This course introduces the core concepts and technologies behind spatial omics, with a strong focus on practical data analysis in R/Bioconductor. Participants will learn how to work with both sequencing- and imaging-based spatial data, apply tidy data principles, and perform key analyses such as spatial differential expression and cell?cell interaction studies.
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/spatial-omics-1 ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/spatial-omics-1 ) 
 
2 -  Spatial Omics Data Analysis: From Raw Data to AI Insights
Dates:  14?16 September 
Instructors: Dr. Sara Jim?nez & Tim Treis (Helmholtz Munich, Germany)
 
This course provides an end-to-end workflow for spatial omics data analysis, from raw data processing and quality control to advanced spatial statistics and AI-based approaches. Participants will explore spatial structure, gene-level patterns, cell?cell communication, and modern deep learning methods, with a strong emphasis on reproducibility and practical application.
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/scverse/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/scverse/ ) 
 
These trainings are ideal for researchers working in single-cell biology, spatial transcriptomics, computational biology, and bioinformatics who want to strengthen their analytical skills in spatial omics.
 
 
Best regards,
Carlo

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Last chance: Modelling Trait Evolution with R
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: Modelling Trait Evolution with R
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Last chance - Discover advanced statistical and computational methods to analyze evolutionary patterns in your data. Modelling and Mapping Trait Evolution is a 2-day seminar livestreaming May 11 and 13 with Renan Maestri (Department of Ecology, Universidade Federal do Rio Grande do Sul). This seminar provides statistical and computational methods for modelling and mapping phenotypic trait evolution on phylogenies, integrating theoretical foundations with practical R implementations. Researchers in genetic data analysis can apply these techniques to infer evolutionary processes, test macroevolutionary hypotheses, and visualize trait changes in their own genetic datasets. The practical R workflows for continuous and categorical trait models, ancestral state estimation, and multi-regime models will enhance the rigor and reproducibility of your genetic research.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/modelling-and-mapping-trait-evolution) to reserve your place before it starts, and feel free to circulate this with colleagues and students!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Gene Set Enrichment Analysis in R course
messages.subject_clean: Gene Set Enrichment Analysis in R course
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
 
Want to turn long gene lists into real biological insight?
 
You can still join us for the Gene Set Enrichment Analysis in R course, starting next week.( 11?15 May) [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/gse-in-r/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/gse-in-r/ )
 
 
This hands-on course will take you from the fundamentals of gene set enrichment to advanced, publication-ready analyses in R. You?ll learn how to run, interpret, and visualize enrichment results across transcriptomics, proteomics, and multi-omics data ? using the tools actually used in current research.
 
>From classic ORA to GSEA, pathway analysis, GO interpretation, and network-based approaches, you?ll build a complete, practical workflow you can immediately apply to your own data.
 
And you are welcome to bring your own dataset.
 
If you work with transcriptomic or multi-omics data, this is the step that makes your results biologically meaningful.
 
Best, Carlo

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D
